description: Backend P4 — Fechar Gaps Finais pós-Landlord Rollout. Continuação do rollout P1→P3 (US-001 a US-020 já entregues). Cobre os itens 1–12 da priorização §8 do GAPS_FINAIS.md: (a) bugs ativos visíveis nos logs do frontend — imagens do /uploads não carregam, GET /api/conversations dá timeout, GET /api/support/tickets dá timeout; (b) endpoints que completam telas já iniciadas — payments multi-mês, Contract.documentStatus, echo do POST ticket; (c) melhorias — landlord/metrics, per-property analytics, Visit.source MANUAL|AI, Notification persistido cross-device (GET + PUT read), expansão do PropertyType enum e amenidades (hasWifi, hasPool). Decisões de produto abertas (thread chat de ticket §1.3/§4.4, TransactionType §6.3, isIdentityVerified §2.6, gráfico mensal §2.2, instrumentação de telemetria) ficam em Open Questions do PRD e NÃO entram neste rollout. Zero quebras nas respostas entregues pelas US-001→US-020. 5 migrations aditivas (documentStatus, Visit.source, Notification model, PropertyType enum +4 valores, hasWifi/hasPool).

userStories.title: Investigate image loading root cause (/uploads 404/CORS)
userStories.description: As backend dev, I need to confirm which of the 3 hypotheses from GAPS_FINAIS.md §1.4 is causing the placeholder-of-house in the landlord 'Gestão de Aluguéis' screen and property detail page, so US-002 fix is targeted.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `ls uploads/` at repo root and confirm which propertyId directories exist and contain .jpg files — list in progress.txt learnings

Pick one property with known images (check prisma/seed.ts or `psql` the PropertyImage table) and curl `http://localhost:3000/uploads/<propertyId>/<file>.jpg` — record status code, Content-Type, and presence/absence of Access-Control-Allow-Origin header in progress.txt
Check `src/app.ts` around line 47: confirm whether `cors()` middleware is applied BEFORE or AFTER `express.static('/uploads', ...)` — document the ordering in progress.txt learnings
Document the confirmed root cause (hypothesis 1=CORS, 2=missing file, 3=Content-Type) in progress.txt — US-002 reads this to pick the fix
NO code changes in this story — investigation only
progress.txt has a clear 'Root cause: <hypothesis N>' line so US-002 can act on it

userStories.title: Apply /uploads fix so property images serve correctly
userStories.description: As a user (landlord or tenant), I want property photos to load instead of the house placeholder on the landlord rentals screen and property detail page.
acceptanceCriteria: Apply the fix identified in US-001 progress.txt (most likely: move `app.use(cors(...))` BEFORE `app.use('/uploads', express.static(...))` in src/app.ts)
If fix is CORS: the cors() options must allow the Flutter web origin (localhost:5173 or whatever ALLOWED_ORIGINS env accepts); fall through for native mobile (no Origin header)

`curl -i http://localhost:3000/uploads/<known-propertyId>/<file>.jpg -H 'Origin: http://localhost:5173'` responds 200 with `Content-Type: image/jpeg` and `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: http://localhost:5173`
If root cause was missing files (hypothesis 2), document the backfill approach in progress.txt and patch the upload handler to log the absolute path it writes to
No regressions in existing multipart upload flow (POST /api/properties and PUT /api/properties/:id with photos[] still write files to uploads/<propertyId>/)
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)

userStories.title: GET /api/support/tickets — user lists own tickets
userStories.description: As an authenticated tenant or landlord, I want to see the list of support tickets I created, so the /support screen stops falling back to local cache and stops timing out in the logs (GAPS_FINAIS.md §1.2, §4.1).
acceptanceCriteria: Add `GET /api/support/tickets` route in `src/routes/supportTicketRoutes.ts` (or existing ticket routes file — grep first) behind authStack (checkJwt + authSyncMiddleware)
Controller filters `where: { userId: req.localUser.id }` and orders `createdAt DESC`
Response: array of `{ id, code, title, description, createdAt, status }` — match the shape the frontend cache already expects per GAPS_FINAIS.md §347
Does NOT expose any other user's tickets — tests/supportTicketsList.test.ts covers: user A sees only their tickets, user B sees only theirs
Uses same `supportTicketService` pattern as existing admin list endpoint (US-019) — extract a shared query helper if needed, don't copy-paste
Follow the `importOriginal` authMiddleware mock pattern noted in archived progress.txt Codebase Patterns when writing tests that assert 401/403
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)

userStories.title: GET /api/conversations — list user conversations
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want to see my conversations on the /chat screen so the listing doesn't depend only on the /resolve 1:1 resolver — fixes the connectionTimeout in GAPS_FINAIS.md §1.1.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `GET /api/conversations` route in conversationRoutes.ts behind authStack
Optional query param `?unreadOnly=true` filters to conversations with at least one message where readAt IS NULL and authorId != req.localUser.id
Response: array of `{ id, counterpartName, counterpartAvatarUrl, lastMessage, lastMessageAt, unread (bool), linkedPropertyId, linkedTenantId }` — per GAPS_FINAIS.md §292-306
For each conversation, resolve counterpart by `Conversation.participants[]` excluding req.localUser.id; if landlord is requester, include `linkedTenantId` (tenant participant's userId)
Ordering: `lastMessageAt DESC` (use Conversation.lastMessageAt if populated by US-006, else fall back to max(Message.createdAt))
Only returns conversations where req.localUser.id is in participants
tests/conversationsList.test.ts covers: empty list, two conversations ordered by lastMessageAt, user filter (user C doesn't see A↔B's conversation)
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)

userStories.title: GET /api/conversations/:id/messages — paginated message history
userStories.description: As a conversation participant, I want to page through message history (reverse-keyset) so the chat screen loads older messages on scroll.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `GET /api/conversations/:id/messages` route behind authStack
Query params: `?before=<messageId>&limit=50`; default limit=50, max=100
When `before` provided: return messages with `createdAt < (SELECT createdAt FROM Message WHERE id = before)`, ordered `createdAt DESC`, limited
When `before` omitted: return the most recent `limit` messages, ordered `createdAt DESC`
Response: array of `{ id, authorId, content, createdAt, readAt }`
403 if `req.localUser.id` is not in `conversation.participants`
404 if the conversation does not exist
tests/conversationMessagesGet.test.ts covers: happy path, pagination via `before`, 403 for non-participant, 404 for missing conversation
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)

userStories.title: POST /api/conversations/:id/messages — send message
userStories.description: As a conversation participant, I want to send a message so the 1:1 chat actually works.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `POST /api/conversations/:id/messages` route behind authStack
Body: `{ content: string }`; validate non-empty and length <= 4000 (return 400 otherwise with existing error envelope)
Creates Message with `authorId = req.localUser.id, content, createdAt = now(), readAt = null`
Same transaction updates `Conversation.lastMessageAt = message.createdAt` (add field to Conversation model if it doesn't exist — hand-authored migration per Codebase Patterns)
Response 201 with the created message `{ id, authorId, content, createdAt, readAt: null }`
403 if req.localUser is not a participant
tests/conversationMessagesPost.test.ts covers: happy path, content validation (empty, too long), 403 for non-participant
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)

userStories.title: GET /api/properties/:propertyId/payments — multi-month history
userStories.description: As a landlord, I want to see the complete payment history for a tenant on the 'Histórico Financeiro' screen instead of the synthetic 1-line fallback (GAPS_FINAIS.md §2.4).
acceptanceCriteria: Add `GET /api/properties/:propertyId/payments?tenantId=:uuid` route behind landlordAuthStack (per existing pattern in propertyRoutes)
Ownership check: 403 if `property.landlordId !== req.localUser.id`; 404 if property doesn't exist
Response: array of `{ period: 'YYYY-MM', amount: number, status: 'PAID'|'AWAITING'|'LATE', paidAt: ISO|null }` ordered `period DESC`

Derive one row per month from `Contract.startDate` up to current month: if a RentalPayment exists for that (propertyId, tenantId, period), use its status+amount+paidAt; else synthesize status=LATE (if past) or AWAITING (if current), amount=contract.monthlyRent, paidAt=null
Deserialize `@db.Decimal(10,2)` monthlyRent → number with `Number(row.field)` per archived Codebase Patterns
tests/propertyPaymentsHistory.test.ts covers: contract with 3 months, 2 paid + 1 late; ownership 403; missing property 404; missing tenantId 400
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)

userStories.title: Contract.documentStatus — migration + GET/PUT wiring
userStories.description: As a landlord, I want a real tenant document status chip (green/orange/red) in 'Meus Inquilinos' instead of the heuristic based on property.status (GAPS_FINAIS.md §2.5).
acceptanceCriteria: Add Prisma enum `ContractDocumentStatus { APPROVED AWAITING_SIGNATURE PENDING_DOCUMENTS }` to schema.prisma
Add field `documentStatus ContractDocumentStatus @default(PENDING_DOCUMENTS)` to Contract model
Hand-author `prisma/migrations/<stamp>_add_contract_document_status/migration.sql` (never run `prisma migrate dev` per archived Codebase Patterns)
Verify with `npx prisma migrate diff --from-empty --to-schema-datamodel prisma/schema.prisma --script` — DDL must match byte-for-byte
`GET /api/contracts` (existing) now includes `documentStatus` in response shape
`PUT /api/contracts/:id` (existing) accepts `documentStatus` in body; validates enum value; restricted to LANDLORD who owns the property linked to the contract
prisma/seed.ts updates existing contract seeds with coherent documentStatus values
tests/contractDocumentStatus.test.ts covers: GET returns the field, PUT updates it, PUT 403 for non-owner, PUT 400 for invalid enum value
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)

userStories.title: Echo title/description in POST /api/support/tickets response
userStories.description: As a frontend dev, I want title and description echoed in the POST ticket response, so the local cache doesn't need to preserve the request body to assemble the ticket (GAPS_FINAIS.md §4.2).
acceptanceCriteria: Modify `POST /api/support/tickets` controller to return `{ id, code, title, description, createdAt, status }` instead of `{ id, code, createdAt }`
Error contract (400, 401) unchanged
Existing tests for POST ticket still pass (update them to assert the new fields)
No change to schema, no migration needed
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)

userStories.title: GET /api/landlord/metrics — dashboard cards
userStories.description: As a landlord, I want the 'Visitas ao perfil' and 'Propostas' cards to show real numbers instead of '—' and a 'métrica ainda não disponível' tooltip (GAPS_FINAIS.md §2.1).
acceptanceCriteria: Add `GET /api/landlord/metrics` route behind landlordAuthStack
Response: `{ profileViews: number, proposalsPending: number, unreadMessages: number }`

`profileViews`: reuse the existing ProfileView table from LL-001 (archived run) — count WHERE landlordId = req.localUser.id AND viewedAt >= now() - interval '30 days'. If LL-001's ProfileView model doesn't exist in the current schema, return 0 and add TODO comment in controller (don't create a new table)
`proposalsPending`: count `Proposal` where `propertyId IN (landlord's properties) AND status = 'PENDING'`
`unreadMessages`: count Message where `conversation.participants` includes landlord, `authorId != landlord`, `readAt IS NULL` — acceptable to return 0 if query is too costly, with TODO comment
tests/landlordMetrics.test.ts covers: empty (all zeros), populated case with seeded ProfileView + Proposal + Message, role check (non-landlord gets 403)
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)

userStories.title: GET /api/properties/:id/analytics — per-property cards
userStories.description: As a landlord, I want the top cards of the 'Análise do Imóvel' screen to show real data (GAPS_FINAIS.md §2.3).
acceptanceCriteria: Add `GET /api/properties/:id/analytics?window=30d|90d|1y` route behind landlordAuthStack; default window=30d
Ownership check: 403 if property.landlordId !== req.localUser.id; 404 if property not found
Response: `{ views, favorites, proposalsTotal, proposalsOpen, visitsScheduled, contactClicks, dailyViews: [{date, count}] }`
`favorites`: count existing Favorite rows for property (if table exists — grep first)
`proposalsTotal`, `proposalsOpen`, `visitsScheduled`: real counts over the window
`views`, `contactClicks`, `dailyViews`: return 0/[] with TODO comment in controller (no telemetry instrumentation exists yet — Open Question #5 in PRD)
Do NOT create new tracking tables — that's an instrumentation PRD, not this one
tests/propertyAnalytics.test.ts covers: 30d window, 90d window, 1y window, ownership 403, default window, invalid window (400)
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)

userStories.title: Visit.source (MANUAL|AI) — migration + endpoint exposure
userStories.description: As a user of the Smart Agenda, I want visually distinct dots for MANUAL vs AI-scheduled visits (GAPS_FINAIS.md §5.1).
acceptanceCriteria: Add enum `VisitSource { MANUAL AI }` to schema.prisma
Add field `source VisitSource @default(MANUAL)` to Visit model
Hand-author `prisma/migrations/<stamp>_add_visit_source/migration.sql` — ALTER TABLE with CHECK constraint matching the enum
Verify DDL byte-for-byte with `prisma migrate diff`
All `GET /api/visits*` endpoints include `source` in response

`POST /api/visits` accepts optional `source` in body; if req.localUser lacks an `ai-agent` scope (which doesn't exist yet — treat as always false), force `source = MANUAL` and ignore the request body value. Add TODO comment referencing Open Question #6
prisma/seed.ts sets `source: 'MANUAL'` explicitly for existing visit seeds
tests/visitSource.test.ts covers: GET includes source, POST defaults to MANUAL, POST with source=AI from non-agent user is downgraded to MANUAL
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)

userStories.title: Notification model + persist on broadcast + GET /api/notifications
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my notification history from any device (not just local cache of the current app install) — GAPS_FINAIS.md §7.1.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Prisma enum `NotificationCategory { update announcement system }` to schema.prisma

Add Prisma model `Notification { id String @id @default(uuid()), userId String, title String, body String, receivedAt DateTime @default(now()), readAt DateTime?, category NotificationCategory @default(announcement), @@index([userId, receivedAt]) }` with FK to User onDelete Cascade
Hand-author migration; verify DDL with `prisma migrate diff`
Modify `POST /api/admin/broadcast` (US-019 of prior rollout) to persist one Notification row per target userId in the same transaction as the FCM dispatch
Add `GET /api/notifications?unreadOnly=true` route behind authStack
Response: array ordered `receivedAt DESC` of `{ id, title, body, receivedAt, read (bool), category }` where `read = (readAt !== null)`
Filter: `userId = req.localUser.id` — tests/notificationsList.test.ts asserts no cross-user leak
tests/notificationsList.test.ts covers: empty list, populated ordered DESC, unreadOnly filter, cross-user isolation
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)

userStories.title: PUT /api/notifications/:id/read — cross-device sync
userStories.description: As a user, I want marking a notification as read on one device to reflect on others (GAPS_FINAIS.md §7.2).
acceptanceCriteria: Add `PUT /api/notifications/:id/read` route behind authStack (depends on Notification model from US-013)
Only the owner can mark (`notification.userId === req.localUser.id`)
Sets `readAt = now()` if still NULL; idempotent — if already set, return 204 without updating
Response: 204 No Content
403 if caller is not the owner; 404 if notification does not exist
tests/notificationMarkRead.test.ts covers: happy path, idempotency, 403 for non-owner, 404 for missing id
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)

userStories.title: Expand PropertyType enum (+KITNET, PENTHOUSE, LAND, COMMERCIAL)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to pick KITNET, PENTHOUSE (Cobertura), LAND (Terreno), or COMMERCIAL (Comercial) when listing or filtering properties, matching the 8 chips in the UI (GAPS_FINAIS.md §6.1).
acceptanceCriteria: Add `KITNET, PENTHOUSE, LAND, COMMERCIAL` to Prisma `enum PropertyType`

Hand-author migration using `ALTER TYPE ... ADD VALUE ...` (one per new variant); verify with `prisma migrate diff`. Note: `ALTER TYPE ADD VALUE` cannot run inside a transaction block — migration.sql must not wrap them in BEGIN/COMMIT
Update Zod/Joi/class-validator schema for `POST /api/properties` and `PUT /api/properties/:id` to accept the new values
Update validation for `GET /api/properties/search?type=` to accept the new values
Update prisma/seed.ts — add at least 1 seed property for each new type
tests/propertyTypeExpansion.test.ts covers: POST with each new type succeeds, PUT with each new type succeeds, search filter by each new type returns seeded row
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)

userStories.title: Property.hasWifi + Property.hasPool amenities + search filters
userStories.description: As a user, I want to filter properties by WiFi or pool, and as a landlord I want to mark these amenities on create/edit (GAPS_FINAIS.md §6.2).
acceptanceCriteria: Add `hasWifi Boolean @default(false)` and `hasPool Boolean @default(false)` to Property model
Hand-author migration; verify DDL byte-for-byte with `prisma migrate diff`
`POST /api/properties` and `PUT /api/properties/:id` accept both fields in body (validation: boolean)
`GET /api/properties/:id` response includes both fields
`GET /api/properties/search` accepts `?hasWifi=true` and `?hasPool=true` query params (combinable with existing filters)
prisma/seed.ts: set hasWifi/hasPool on some seed properties to enable realistic search filter demos

tests/propertyAmenities.test.ts covers: POST with amenities, PUT toggles amenities, GET returns amenities, search filter ?hasWifi=true returns only wifi properties, combined ?hasWifi=true&hasPool=true AND-composes
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)
